appState.researchQuestion: How does the assignment mark relate to the final exam mark?

current.explanation: The research question asks how a student’s assignment mark relates to the final exam mark. When the assignment mark is zero the model predicts an average exam mark of 11. The 95% confidence interval runs from 4 to 18. For every additional one-point rise in the assignment mark, the exam mark rises by about 3 points (95% confidence interval 2.5 to 3.5). This linear relationship accounts for about 51% of the variation in exam marks. Overall, on average, higher assignment marks are associated with higher final exam marks.

runs.explanation: How does the assignment mark relate to the final exam mark? For a student whose assignment score is 0, the model predicts an average exam score of 11 (95% confidence interval 4 to 18). Each additional one-point increase in the assignment score raises the expected exam score by about 3 points. The 95% confidence interval runs from 2.5 to 3.5. About 51% of the variation in exam scores is explained by this relationship. Overall, on average, higher assignment marks are strongly associated with higher final exam marks, with each point on the assignment contributing roughly three more points to the exam.
